title: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel
raw_response: {"rating": 3.6, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is a news report that leans Right. The author repeatedly frames Donald Trump positively (including labeling him a whistleblower) and attacks his opponents as spreading lies about his Epstein/Maxwell ties, without offering balancing context. Editorial choices like a related link implicating Democrats and an exculpatory closing further support a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author labels Trump as a “whistleblower” in their own prose, a laudatory characterization that goes beyond neutral reporting; (2) The author asserts that a “simple fact” disproves Trump’s critics and says his opponents have spread “lies and falsehoods,” an overtly adversarial framing toward Trump’s political opponents; (3) The related-link headline selected by the author/editor steers readers to a narrative tying Democrats to Epstein, reinforcing a partisan takeaway; (4) The headline and lede spotlight Trump as having condemned Epstein/Maxwell long ago, presenting him as prescient and distancing him from wrongdoing; (5) The concluding sentence emphasizes Trump’s consistent denials and that he has not been accused by victims, offering exculpatory framing without countervailing context; (6) Omission: no substantive counterpoints about Trump’s past social proximity to Epstein are included, despite their relevance to a balanced account."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report that leans Right. The author repeatedly frames Donald Trump positively (including labeling him a whistleblower) and attacks his opponents as spreading lies about his Epstein/Maxwell ties, without offering balancing context. Editorial choices like a related link implicating Democrats and an exculpatory closing further support a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author labels Trump as a “whistleblower” in their own prose, a laudatory characterization that goes beyond neutral reporting; (2) The author asserts that a “simple fact” disproves Trump’s critics and says his opponents have spread “lies and falsehoods,” an overtly adversarial framing toward Trump’s political opponents; (3) The related-link headline selected by the author/editor steers readers to a narrative tying Democrats to Epstein, reinforcing a partisan takeaway; (4) The headline and lede spotlight Trump as having condemned Epstein/Maxwell long ago, presenting him as prescient and distancing him from wrongdoing; (5) The concluding sentence emphasizes Trump’s consistent denials and that he has not been accused by victims, offering exculpatory framing without countervailing context; (6) Omission: no substantive counterpoints about Trump’s past social proximity to Epstein are included, despite their relevance to a balanced account.
